title: Staffing Firm Placement Capacity Score
subtitle: Find the bottleneck between your team size and your placement volume

persona_selector.text: Which best describes your staffing firm right now?

answers.label: Boutique firm, 10-20 employees, founder still in daily ops
Growing firm, 21-40 employees, building out management layer
Established firm, 41-75 employees, multiple desks or verticals
Multi-location or multi-vertical firm scaling aggressively

questions.text: When a new job order comes in, how long before a recruiter is actively working it?

answers.label: More than 24 hours, often longer
Within 12 to 24 hours most of the time
Within 4 to 8 hours on a good day
Within 1 to 2 hours consistently
Under 30 minutes, automated routing in place

questions.text: How is job order information captured from clients?

answers.label: Phone calls and notes scattered across inboxes
Email threads that someone manually transcribes later
A form or template, filled out inconsistently
A standard intake form feeding our ATS directly
Structured intake with auto parsing into ATS fields

questions.text: How do you qualify whether a new client or job order is worth pursuing?

answers.label: We take almost everything, sort it out later
Gut feel from whoever picks up the call
A rough checklist the team mostly follows
A documented scoring rubric applied to every order
Automated scoring on fit, fee, urgency, and fill probability

questions.text: How often do job orders get dropped, forgotten, or worked twice?

answers.label: Weekly, it is a known problem
A few times a month
Occasionally, usually during busy weeks
Rarely, our tracking catches most of it
Never, every order has a clear owner and status

low.headline: Your intake is leaking orders before they ever reach a recruiter
low.text: Right now, new job orders are stuck in inboxes, voicemails, and informal conversations. By the time a recruiter starts sourcing, the client has already called your competitor. This is the single fastest fix in most firms because it does not require new clients, just faster handling of the ones you already have.
steps: Build one standardized intake form that every client request flows into, regardless of channel
Assign a single owner to every new order within 30 minutes of receipt with an automated routing rule
Create a simple qualification rubric covering fee, urgency, and fillability, and apply it to every order before work begins

mid.headline: Intake works but it is not fast or consistent enough to scale
mid.text: You have the basics in place, but inconsistency is costing you placements. Recruiters are still chasing down missing information, and qualification varies by whoever handles the call. Tightening this stage will unlock capacity your team already has without hiring anyone new.
steps: Audit the last 30 days of intake and identify which fields are missing most often, then make them required
Automate order routing based on desk, vertical, or recruiter specialty instead of manual assignment
Standardize your qualification rubric into a scorecard that surfaces priority orders at the top of every recruiter queue

high.headline: Intake is a strength, now make it a competitive weapon
high.text: Your intake process is already faster than most competitors. The next level is using speed and structure to win more of the orders you pursue and capture better data for every downstream decision.
steps: Add client-facing intake confirmation that sets expectations on timelines and next steps automatically
Layer predictive fill probability scoring onto every new order so recruiters focus on the highest yield work first
Feed intake data into quarterly client scorecards to drive renewal conversations and fee increases

questions.text: When a new job order lands, where do you look for candidates first?

answers.label: We start sourcing from scratch on job boards
We check LinkedIn and our ATS, but data is stale
We search our ATS first, then supplement with boards
We have curated talent pools by role and skill
We already have qualified candidates pre-matched to common roles

questions.text: How fresh is the data in your candidate database?

answers.label: Most records are over a year out of date
We refresh contact info when we reach out
We update active candidates quarterly
Automated reminders keep top candidates current
Database is continuously enriched and verified by automation

questions.text: How do you stay in touch with placed and passive candidates?

answers.label: We do not, we contact them when we need them
Occasional check-ins when a recruiter remembers
A quarterly email blast to the full list
Segmented nurture campaigns by skill or industry
Automated personalized touchpoints with engagement tracking

questions.text: How long does it typically take to present the first qualified candidate on a new role?

answers.label: Over a week, sometimes longer
3 to 5 business days
Around 48 hours
Within 24 hours
Same day, from existing pipeline

low.headline: Every new order is a cold start, and that is killing your speed
low.text: Starting from scratch on every role means you are competing on sourcing effort instead of relationships. Firms that win on speed have a pipeline before the order arrives. This is where recruiters burn the most time with the least leverage.
steps: Identify your 5 most placed roles and build evergreen talent pools for each one with at least 20 pre-qualified candidates
Implement a monthly database hygiene process to flag stale records and trigger re-engagement outreach
Start a lightweight nurture campaign to placed candidates and silver medalists so they stay warm between opportunities

mid.headline: Your pipeline exists but it is not doing enough work for you
mid.text: You have candidates on file, but too much of the value stays locked in individual recruiters' heads and inboxes. The goal is making your database behave like a living asset that produces ready candidates before a recruiter even looks.
steps: Segment your candidate database by skill cluster and engagement recency so sourcing queries return usable results in under 60 seconds
Automate data enrichment on top candidates to keep contact info, current role, and skills current without recruiter effort
Launch segmented nurture sequences tied to candidate specialty so passive talent stays engaged month to month

high.headline: Pipeline is a real asset, now compound it
high.text: You are already presenting candidates faster than competitors. The next move is turning that advantage into predictable revenue by measuring pipeline health as carefully as you measure billings.
steps: Track pipeline coverage ratio by role type so you know which verticals are under-supplied before clients ask
Build automated candidate-to-job matching suggestions that surface the top 5 matches the moment an order is created
Layer AI-powered candidate scoring on engagement signals to predict which passive candidates will respond this quarter

questions.text: How long does a recruiter spend qualifying a single candidate for a role?

answers.label: 45 minutes or more per candidate
Around 30 minutes per candidate
15 to 20 minutes per candidate
Under 10 minutes with a structured process
Under 5 minutes, supported by automated pre-screens

questions.text: How do you match candidates to open roles?

answers.label: Recruiter memory and manual keyword searches
Boolean searches in the ATS, run fresh every time
Saved searches and tagged candidate pools
Structured match scoring on skills, comp, and location
AI-assisted matching surfaces top candidates automatically

questions.text: How consistent is the quality of candidates you submit to clients?

answers.label: Highly variable, depends entirely on the recruiter
Mostly okay, with frequent client pushback
Reasonable, with occasional misses
Strong, with a clear submission standard
Consistently high, backed by a scoring rubric

questions.text: How do you pre-screen candidates before presenting them?

answers.label: We rarely pre-screen, we submit and see
Quick phone call, unstructured
Phone screen with a rough set of questions
Structured intake with documented criteria
Automated intake plus structured recruiter interview

low.headline: Qualification is eating your recruiters alive
low.text: If every candidate takes 30 to 45 minutes to qualify, your team is capped at a handful of real conversations per day. That is a ceiling on placements that no amount of hustle can break through. Cut qualification time and you immediately add capacity.
steps: Build a standard pre-screen questionnaire that candidates complete before the recruiter call, covering must-have criteria
Create a submission rubric so every candidate submitted to a client hits the same baseline of qualification
Replace open-ended recruiter calls with a 10-minute structured interview script tied to the job order requirements

mid.headline: Your qualification process works but it is not fast enough to scale
mid.text: Recruiters have a process, but it is still doing work that automation and structure could handle. The goal is to let humans focus on judgment calls and relationships, not data collection.
steps: Automate the first layer of pre-screening with a candidate-facing form that captures availability, comp, and hard requirements
Standardize match scoring across skills, location, comp, and culture so every candidate gets a comparable score
Build a submission quality checklist that every recruiter applies before sending candidates to clients

high.headline: You are fast and accurate, now lock in the advantage
high.text: Your qualification speed is a real differentiator. The next step is layering intelligence on top so your best recruiters' judgment scales across the whole team.
steps: Deploy AI-assisted matching that surfaces the top 5 candidates ranked by fit score the moment an order opens
Capture structured interview data that feeds back into your matching model to improve recommendations over time
Benchmark time-to-submit and interview-to-hire ratios weekly so you can spot process drift before it costs placements

questions.text: How are onboarding documents, contracts, and compliance handled?

answers.label: Manually, email back and forth with PDFs
Shared drive with templates, manual tracking
E-signature tools, manual reminders
Automated onboarding workflow with status tracking
Fully automated compliance with audit trails

questions.text: How much recruiter or admin time is spent on data entry each week?

answers.label: 10 or more hours per person
5 to 10 hours per person
2 to 5 hours per person
Under 2 hours per person
Near zero, systems sync automatically

questions.text: How do invoicing, timesheets, and payroll flow through your firm?

answers.label: Manual collection and chasing every pay period
Spreadsheets with manual review
Dedicated tool but heavy manual reconciliation
Integrated systems with automated approvals
End-to-end automation with exception-only review

questions.text: How often do back office errors cause client or candidate issues?

answers.label: Weekly, it is a constant firefight
Several times a month
Once a month or so
A few times a year
Very rarely, caught before they escalate

low.headline: Back office drag is stealing hours from your highest paid people
low.text: When recruiters and admins spend 10 plus hours a week on data entry and paper chasing, you are paying placement-quality people to do entry-level work. Every hour here is an hour not spent on clients, candidates, or revenue.
steps: Move onboarding, contracts, and e-signatures into a single automated workflow with status visibility for every stakeholder
Audit the top 5 data entry tasks consuming recruiter time and automate or delegate each one within 60 days
Integrate your ATS, invoicing, and timesheet tools so data flows without manual re-entry between systems

mid.headline: Your back office is functional but still too hands on
mid.text: You have tools in place, but they are not talking to each other well enough. The cost shows up in small errors, manual reconciliation, and a constant low level of admin work that never quite ends.
steps: Map every manual handoff between systems and replace the top 3 with direct integrations or automated workflows
Implement automated compliance tracking with alerts for expiring documents, licenses, and certifications
Build an exception-based review process for invoicing and payroll so staff only touch what actually needs attention

high.headline: Back office is efficient, turn it into a margin advantage
high.text: You have controlled the drag most firms live with. The upside now is using that efficiency to support faster growth without proportional headcount increases.
steps: Track operational cost per placement monthly and set targets that tighten as volume grows
Build self-service dashboards for clients and contractors to cut inbound admin questions by at least half
Redeploy saved admin capacity into account management and candidate experience, where it drives new revenue

questions.text: How quickly can you see real-time placement activity across your team?

answers.label: I ask people and wait for answers
Weekly meeting recaps and manual reports
ATS reports, run on request
Live dashboards updated daily
Real-time dashboards with alerting on key metrics

questions.text: Do you know your cost per placement and recruiter profitability?

answers.label: No, we estimate revenue and hope
Roughly, based on annual totals
We calculate quarterly
Monthly with reasonable accuracy
Real-time, per recruiter and per desk

questions.text: How do you forecast placements and revenue for next month?

answers.label: Gut feel and last month's numbers
Open order count times a rough close rate
Pipeline review with each recruiter
Weighted pipeline with stage-based probabilities
Data-driven forecast using historical close rates per stage

questions.text: When something slips, how fast do you know about it?

answers.label: When a client calls upset
When a recruiter finally flags it
Next weekly team meeting
Within 24 hours via dashboards
Immediately, automated alerts trigger on risk signals

low.headline: You are flying blind, and it is costing you placements you never see
low.text: Without real-time visibility, you are making decisions on gut feel and last month's memory. By the time you notice a slipping order or an underperforming recruiter, the damage is already done. Visibility is cheap compared to what you lose without it.
steps: Build a single placement activity dashboard covering submissions, interviews, offers, and placements updated at least daily
Calculate cost per placement and revenue per recruiter monthly, and share the numbers with your leadership team
Set automated alerts for stalled orders, aging candidates, and missed client follow-ups so issues surface the day they appear

mid.headline: You have data but it is not driving decisions fast enough
mid.text: You know roughly how the business is doing, but the reporting cycle is too slow to change outcomes in the current month. Tightening the loop between data and action is where growth firms break away from stagnating ones.
steps: Move from monthly reporting to weekly live dashboards with activity, pipeline, and close rate metrics by recruiter
Implement a weighted pipeline forecast with stage-based close probabilities tied to 12 months of historical data
Create recruiter scorecards so individual performance trends are visible within the week, not after the quarter closes

high.headline: Data is a strength, now use it to make bigger bets
high.text: You have the visibility most firms lack. The advantage now is using that data to decide faster and bigger than competitors on hiring, pricing, and vertical expansion.
steps: Build predictive models on which orders are most likely to close and which candidates are most likely to accept
Run quarterly profitability reviews by vertical and desk to redirect investment toward highest return segments
Use data to justify premium pricing by showing clients placement speed, quality, and retention benchmarks versus market

cta.headline: Ready to turn your weakest category into your biggest revenue lever?
cta.description: Book a 30-minute strategy call and we will walk through your score, pinpoint the biggest bottleneck, and map out a concrete automation plan to unlock placement capacity with the team you already have.
